output.title: Platform (June & Fall Launch 2025 Updates)
output.textPreview:
Platform (June & Fall Launch 2025 Updates)
Platform Page: SEO
Contentful Platform
Power your brand with Contentful’s digital experience platform. A smarter, faster way to build, optimize, and scale experiences across every channel
Platform Page: Hero (June 2025)
Contentful Platform (June 2025)
One content platform, infinite flexibility
Power your brand with Contentful’s digital experience platform at your core. A smarter, faster way to build, optimize, and scale experiences across every channel and any team.
(Global) Contact sales button
Contact sales
Internal
View plans (generic)
View plans
Internal
Platform Page: Section Group (Basics) (June 2025)
Platform Page: Product Hero (June 2025)
Platform Hero: 3-Up Copy Block (June 2025)
Made to move at lightspeed
Streamlined workflows and AI-powered tools make content creation, personalization, and publishing faster for every team.
Scale across digital channels
From peak traffic to global expansion, our infrastructure delivers fast, consistent content — on key channels where your brand lives.
Composable marketing stack
API-first and cloud-native, teams can plug in tools they love — DAMs, CRMs, commerce platforms — and create their ideal tech stack.
left
AI Tout - Endless possibilities powered by AI
Endless possibilities powered by AI
Empower your teams to automate content creation, streamline workflows, and deliver personalized, on-brand experiences — all while boosting efficiency and consistency.
CTA: Explore AI at Contentful
Expl...

calls.purpose: initialize Contentful management client for live trace capture
resolve Contentful space before fetching the entry
resolve Contentful environment before fetching the entry
fetch the full page body through the Contentful delivery APIs

calls.purpose: build query embedding for dynamic few-shot retrieval

input.textPreview: Platform (June & Fall Launch 2025 Updates) | page | products/platform | Personalization | Platform (June & Fall Launch 2025 Updates) Platform (June & Fall Launc...

calls.purpose: derive factual taxonomy fields from content and retrieval context
derive subjective taxonomy fields from content and fact summary
export run trace to OTEL collectors
store structured classification trace in Postgres
flush pending LangSmith traces produced by traceable steps

wiring.description: management API resolves the crawl root before CDA deep crawl
deep-crawled Contentful text becomes classifier input
heuristic signals are enriched by the NLP sidecar
page summary becomes an embedding query for few-shot retrieval
query embedding is used to retrieve similar corrected examples
retrieved examples are injected into the fact-stage prompt
fact output is summarized into the subjective-stage prompt
final result is exported to OTEL collectors
final result is persisted into classification_runs
pending LangSmith traces are flushed after classification completes

output.promptPreview:
You are the factual stage of the Contentful taxonomy pipeline.
Prompt version: 2026-03-19-chain-v4

Return only structured JSON matching the schema.
Do not reason about buyer psychology or marketing subjectivity in this stage.


Focus only on objective, evidentiary classification fields:
- assetType
- assetSubType
- schemaType
- product
- topic
- useCases
- industry
- companySize
- region
- language
- usageRights
- event
- eventType
- season
- yearPublished

Rules:
- Prefer explicit signals, URL structure, company enrichment, and grounded content evidence.
- Use exact allowed labels where provided.
- Keep multi-select fields tight: choose at most 3 values by default, and at most 4 only for genuinely broad educational TOFU content.
- Do not invent product labels or taxonomy values.
- Generic horizontal platform product pages should usually resolve to product=Platform, topic=Headless CMS, useCases=Digital experiences, and industry=Software, IT & Technology.
- On generic platform pages, do not expand to AI, Personalization, General business, or Experimentation from secondary body mentions alone. Title, slug, and primary headings carry more weight than buried feature blurbs.
- Migration and Replatforming belong in topic, not useCases.
- If Personalization is already captured by the product field, avoid duplicating it in topic/useCases unless the page is truly about personalization as a broader concept rather than the product.
- Season is only for time-bound campaigns or event-like content. Never use Season for evergreen pages.
- Webinar pages stay Webinar even when they are live, on-demand, recap, recording, or registration pages. Event is only for physical in-person events.
- GDPR references imply Global relevance, not EMEA-only targeting.
- If a field is marked as an override in the signal block, copy it exactly.
- Confidence must reflect the evidence ceiling in Section 0.



━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SECTION 0 — EVIDENCE QUALITY (READ THIS BEFORE ASSIGNING ANY CONFIDENCE)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Available body text: 32982 chars (after title stripped)
Headings detected:   240
Evidence level:      FULL BODY — sufficient text for confident classification

CONFIDENCE RUBRIC — you MUST calibrate your confidence based on what evidence you actually have:
  TITLE ONLY       → cap ALL semantic field confidence at 0.60 — you are inferring from a title, not reading content
  TITLE + HEADINGS → cap ALL semantic field confidence at 0.74 — limited evidence
  PARTIAL BODY     → cap ALL semantic field confidence at 0.87 — reasonable but incomplete
  FULL BODY        → no cap — full confidence allowed

YOUR CURRENT EVIDENCE CEILING: 97% for topic, useCases, jobLevel, jobFunction, industry, audience

MANDATORY: If you are working from partial body text, state this explicitly in the reasoning field. Do NOT report confidence above 97% for any semantic field — a high confidence score on sparse content is a misrepresentation.


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SECTION 1 — PRE-COMPUTED SIGNALS (ground truth, do not contradict unless you have overwhelming evidence)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Content Type:       page [ground truth — use for schema/format decisions]
URL Pattern:        product
Is Product Page:    YES
Has Demo CTA:       YES — get started, Contact sales
Has Pricing:        YES
Has FAQ structure:  YES
Has Video content:  YES
Has Download:       no
Has Step-by-step:   no
Detected Language:  EN
Mentioned Products: Platform, Personalization
Mentioned Brands:   suppressed — company enrichment disabled for this content type
NLP Provider:       gliner+zero-shot (external)
NLP Intents:        Start Trial (89%), View Pricing (88%), Read Documentation (87%), Talk to Sales (87%), Request Demo (86%), Read FAQ (85%)
NLP Entities:       company:Contentful (86%), company:Contentful (97%), company:Contentful (91%), company:Contentful (97%), technology:DAMs (57%), technology:AI (94%), technology:AI (9...

You are the subjective stage of the Contentful taxonomy pipeline.
Prompt version: 2026-03-19-chain-v4

Return only structured JSON matching the schema.
Do not re-decide the objective facts unless the supplied fact block is obviously impossible.


Focus only on subjective or audience-dependent fields:
- jobLevel
- jobFunction
- audience
- funnelStage
- competitivePositioning
- reasoning

Rules:
- Treat the fact classification block as locked context unless the content clearly contradicts it.
- Resolve funnelStage and jobLevel independently from the same content evidence. They may correlate, but neither field determines the other.
- Funnel stage is about buyer journey intent, CTA strength, and page structure.
- Job level is about the seniority and strategic depth of the content's intended reader.
- Broad educational guides often span multiple non-executive levels, but strategic or executive-oriented educational content can still target VP or C-Level.
- Case studies are Consideration (MOFU), not BOFU.
- Transformational or strategic content should skew Director and above.
- Funnel stage must follow CTA and page-structure evidence before tone-based inference.
- Audience: only select if the content specifically addresses a group. If it doesn't target any particular audience, leave EMPTY. Do not default to "Prospect".
- reasoning: Write ONE LINE PER FIELD for EVERY field below. Put each on its own line (use \n).
  Format: "fieldName: [TAG] value — why"

  FACT FIELDS (from LOCKED FACT CLASSIFICATION above — explain why each was chosen):
  assetType: [LOCKED] or [AI] — why
  assetSubType: [LOCKED] or [AI] — why
  schemaType: [LOCKED] or [AI] — why
  product: [AI] — what products are mentioned in the content
  topic: [AI] — what themes dominate the content
  useCases: [AI] or [METADATA] — what outcomes are discussed
  industry: [AI] or [ENRICHMENT] — cite company vertical or content language
  companySize: [AI] or [ENRICHMENT] — cite revenue data or company scale
  region: [SIGNAL] or [AI] — from signal or content
  language: [SIGNAL] — from signal
  usageRights: [SIGNAL] — from signal

  SUBJECTIVE FIELDS (your decisions in this stage):
  funnelStage: [LOCKED] or [AI] — why
  jobLevel: [AI] — what evidence targets these levels
  jobFunction: [AI] — what roles are addressed in the content
  audience: [AI] — who is this for, or "left blank — no specific audience"
  competitivePositioning: [AI] — competitors named? what positioning?

  Use [METADATA] tag for any field that came from EXISTING ENTRY METADATA.
  EVERY field must appear on its own line. Do not combine fields into paragraphs.
- Confidence must reflect the evidence ceiling in Section 0.



━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SECTION 0 — EVIDENCE QUALITY (READ THIS BEFORE ASSIGNING ANY CONFIDENCE)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

CONFIDENCE RUBRIC — you MUST calibrate your confidence based on what evidence you actually have:
  TITLE ONLY       → cap ALL semantic field confidence at 0.60 — you are inferring from a title, not reading content
  TITLE + HEADINGS → cap ALL semantic field confidence at 0.74 — limited evidence
  PARTIAL BODY     → cap ALL semantic field confidence at 0.87 — reasonable but incomplete
  FULL BODY        → no cap — full confidence allowed

YOUR CURRENT EVIDENCE CEILING: 97% for topic, useCases, jobLevel, jobFunction, industry, audience

MANDATORY: If you are working from partial body text, state this explicitly in the reasoning field. Do NOT report confidence above 97% for any semantic field — a high confidence score on sparse content is a misrepresentation.

output.reasoning:
jobLevel: [AI] Director, VP, C-Level — The content discusses platform updates and strategic capabilities like scaling across digital channels and a composable marketing stack, which typically appeal to senior leadership.
jobFunction: [AI] Marketing, Digital, Digital strategists, Product — The focus on digital experiences, personalization, and platform capabilities directly aligns with the responsibilities of marketing, digital, and product teams.
audience: [AI]  — no specific audience is directly addressed; the content is broadly about the platform's capabilities.
funnelStage: [AI] Evaluation/Engagement (BOFU) — The presence of a "Platform Page" with "Contact sales" and "View plans" CTAs, along with detailed feature headings and product descriptions, indicates a focus on users evaluating the platform for purchase.
competitivePositioning: [AI] none — no competitors are mentioned in the provided text, and no specific positioning against competitors is stated.

[info] Semantic calibration gaps — topic, jobLevel, jobFunction, useCases, funnelStage, industry still lack field-level data backing at this confidence band.

wiring.description: management API resolves the crawl root before CDA deep crawl
deep-crawled Contentful text becomes classifier input
heuristic signals are enriched by the NLP sidecar
page summary becomes an embedding query for few-shot retrieval
query embedding is used to retrieve similar corrected examples
retrieved examples are injected into the fact-stage prompt
fact output is summarized into the subjective-stage prompt
final result is exported to OTEL collectors
final result is persisted into classification_runs
pending LangSmith traces are flushed after classification completes
